channel.title: The Brussels Times
channel.description: Latest Brussels posts from The Brussels Times.

item.title: Brussels' Cinema Palace evacuated following smoke caused by car doing doughnuts
item.description: The interior was filled with smoke and the smell of burning.

item.title: Strike disrupts public transport in Brussels: Which lines are running?
item.description: Due to picket lines, the number of services running is limited, as drivers are unable to leave the depot.

item.title: 'For Flemish people': Flanders considers restricting Dutch-language education in Brussels
item.description: "With all due respect, but Dutch-language education in Brussels is primarily intended for the Flemish people living in Brussels."

item.title: 'Shocking lack of forethought': Saint-Gilles transport chaos sparks anger among residents
item.description: "They have announced four tram closures, and they are just expecting people to accept this until April 2027? That's unreasonable and shows a shocking lack of forethought."

item.title: Feeling lonely in Brussels? Grab an empty chair
item.description: "I want people to know that there will always be someone sitting in a café with an empty chair for them."

item.title: Strike affects public transport in Brussels
item.description: The Brussels government imposed a hiring freeze on public transport company Stib. As a result, the workers have stepped into action, with the full scale of the boycott unknown.

item.title: Chilling with Magritte: Brussels museums may open as free cooling spaces during heatwaves
item.description: Access would be activated as soon as the RMI issues a code yellow, orange, or red for extreme heat.

item.title: So long, Place Misère? Redesign of hated Meiser roundabout moves forward after years of delays
item.description: Due to the constant stream of traffic and inefficient layout the square is seen as one of the city's most dangerous intersections.

item.title: Six people evacuated in Brussels apartment fire
item.description: A fire broke out in an eighth-floor flat in Anderlecht, prompting swift response from the Brussels fire service. The cause of the fire is unknown.

item.title: Public housing organisation's failures put government to test
item.description: A second warning was issued for the organisation Foyer Anderlechtois to iron out irregularities and internal dysfunction. However, little has changed. What comes next?

item.title: 20 Brussels officials fined for lack of transparency
item.description: Over 300 public officials failed to report their income accurately - 27 of them failed to alter their submission even after an initial warning was sent their way.

item.title: Illegal dumping turns Avenue Louise pedestrian tunnel into rubbish hotspot
item.description: The tunnel has been inaccessible since the end of 2025 after it was closed by mayoral order because of unsanitary conditions, drug use and vandalism.

item.title: Nine Brussels puppies in need of 'forever homes' after being separated from their mother
item.description: The American Staffordshire Terrier puppies, currently six weeks old, will be ready to join their new families from 13 July.

item.title: Could Brussels become a '15-minute city’ and how might it benefit residents?
item.description: The concept has been hailed as a framework to improve quality of life, public health and sustainability, but the capital's transport nightmares and uneven resources risk keeping the dream out of reach.

item.title: What to do in Brussels this weekend: 3 - 5 July
item.description: From one of Europe's biggest music festivals to centuries-old traditions in Grand Place, there are plenty of ways to make the most of the first weekend of July.

item.title: Brussels will soon tax electric cars parked at charging stations for too long
item.description: Drivers will only face this fee if their car remains plugged into a terminal for more than six hours between 09:00 and 22:00.

item.title: Cinquantenaire Park 'never looked so clean', responds US ambassador
item.description: According to the US ambassador Bill White, the Cinquantenaire Park was left in a tidy state despite reports of damage to the nearby museum from the fireworks.

item.title: Man seriously injured after balcony fall in Brussels city centre
item.description: A man was seriously injured after falling from a balcony on Boulevard Maurice Lemonnier early on Friday morning.

item.title: Infamous Clémenceau metro shooters in Brussels jailed for over 10 years
item.description: Images of the young man holding and firing an AK-47 inside a Brussels Metro station were circulated across the world.

item.title: Investigation into damage at Cinquantenaire Park following US July 4th party
item.description: The celebration, marking the 250th anniversary of the United States, took place under the triumphal arch of the Cinquantenaire Park in Brussels.
